outbound_official.notes: Israel's official outbound visa table for Israeli ordinary-passport holders (Darkon) is published by the Israeli government at gov.il as 'The State of Israel Tourist Visa Table' (ISR_Visa_Abroad_Eng.pdf), last updated 09.01.2026. This is the origin-side analog of India's mea.gov.in/vffin. The document lists all countries alphabetically with visa requirements, fees, and exemptions for Israeli citizens going abroad. The gov.il BlobFolder domain returns HTTP 403 to all automated scrapers, preventing extraction of the country list at time of writing (2026-06-14); the document URL and last-updated date were confirmed via Google search index snippet. Country-level detail should be populated by downloading the PDF directly from the official URL.

eta.notes: ETA-IL (Electronic Travel Authorization for Israel) is mandatory as of 1 January 2025 for all nationals of visa-exempt countries before travel to Israel. Cost: NIS 25 (~USD 7). Valid for up to 2 years or until passport expires, whichever is first. Multiple trips permitted within validity. Apply at israel-entry.piba.gov.il. Exemptions from ETA-IL apply to: Israeli citizens, holders of Israeli visas, diplomatic/service/official passport holders who are separately exempt, and certain recognized categories per PIBA.

visa_policy.notes: Israel introduced the ETA-IL system on 1 January 2025, replacing straightforward visa-free entry for all nationals of visa-exempt countries. The official tourist visa table (2022_Foreign_Visa_to_Israel.pdf, updated 07.04.2022) is the primary source for inbound exemptions. A reciprocal outbound table (ISR_Visa_Abroad_Eng.pdf, updated 09.01.2026) was also verified. Colombia's visa exemption was removed effective June/July 2025 due to diplomatic tensions. Seychelles gained exemption via agreement signed 11 January 2024. Kosovo's exemption status changed from required (per 2022 table) to exempt by approximately May 2025. Tourist visa exemption applies to ordinary/national passports only unless otherwise noted; official passports follow separate rules per conditional_access.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic, service, and official passports of all countries are exempt from ETA-IL per the official PIBA ETA-IL page. The official Tourist Visa Table confirms that most countries' official passport holders are either already exempt or additionally exempted even when national passport holders require a visa. However, note that some countries' official passports still require a visa (per individual entries in the tourist visa table).

Nationals of these countries require a visa on ordinary passports but are exempt on diplomatic or service (official) passports. Sourced from the official Israeli MFA Tourist Visa Table (2022_Foreign_Visa_to_Israel.pdf). China note: 'Diplomatic/Service Passports exempted for 90 days'. BIH (Bosnia Herzegovina) removed from this list: the 2022 tourist visa table shows BIH national as 'req.' and official as 'exe.' — Bosnia should be in this list; added in corrections. Turkey: 2022 table shows national as 'req.' and official as 'exe.' — confirmed.
Bosnia Herzegovina nationals require a visa on ordinary passports but are exempt on official passports per 2022 official tourist visa table (national: req., official: exe.).

Nationals of Kenya, Rwanda, and Vietnam require a visa on ordinary and service passports, but are exempt on diplomatic passports only. Kenya remark: 'Exe only for diplomatic Passport'. Rwanda remark: 'Only for diplomatic passport'. Vietnam remark: 'Only for Diplomatic passports'. Sourced from the official Israeli MFA Tourist Visa Table (2022_Foreign_Visa_to_Israel.pdf).

cbi.notes: Israel does not offer a Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program. There is no official government program to obtain Israeli citizenship through investment. Citizenship is primarily obtained through the Law of Return (for Jewish persons and eligible relatives), naturalization (typically after 3 years of residency and meeting Hebrew language requirements), or birth. Sources: gov.il official pages on citizenship confirm no CBI program exists.

programs.name: B/5 Investor Visa (US-Israel Treaty Investor)
programs.notes: Available ONLY to US citizens (and their key employees and immediate family) under Israeli Government Resolutions 1528 (March 2014) and 2243 (January 2017). Allows temporary residence and work in Israel to develop and manage a business. The investor must hold at least 50% stake in the business. No fixed minimum investment amount is stated in the official procedure; practical guidance suggests approximately NIS 800,000-1,000,000 (~USD 220,000-275,000). Initial visa period: 2 years, renewable annually with no stated maximum while business requirements are met. Does not provide a direct path to permanent residency or citizenship.

programs.name: Visas for Foreign High-Tech Experts Incentive Program
programs.eligibility: Foreign high-tech experts employed or contracted by Israeli high-tech companies authorized by the Israel Innovation Authority. Expert's salary must be at least double the average wage in Israel.
programs.processing_time: Expedited — work permit can be issued within a few business days for recognized high-tech companies
programs.notes: Launched in 2018 by the Israel Innovation Authority together with the Population and Immigration Authority. Short-term track: up to 90 days through an expedited process. Long-term track: work permit valid up to 1 year, extendable by another year. Maximum stay under long-term working visa for experts: up to 63 months.

programs.name: Innovation Visa (B/2 Innovation Visa for Foreign Entrepreneurs)
programs.eligibility: Foreign entrepreneurs in the technological field seeking to develop innovative ideas in Israel under the support of the Israel Innovation Authority
programs.notes: Pilot program developed by Israel's Ministry of Economy in collaboration with the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the Population and Immigration Authority, and the National Authority of Innovation. Grants a B/2 innovation visa for up to 2 years.

validation_notes: REMOVED Colombia (COL) from visa_free: Colombia's visa exemption for entry to Israel was terminated effective June/July 2025 following diplomatic rupture. Source: Fragomen advisory + Israeli embassy Colombia page (embassies.gov.il/colombia) confirming B-2 visa now required.

ADDED Seychelles (SYC) to visa_free: Bilateral Visa Exemption Agreement signed 11 January 2024 between Israel and Seychelles. Was absent from the record despite being official since Jan 2024. Source: Seychelles MFA official announcement (mfa.gov.sc).
ADDED Saint Lucia (LCA) to visa_free: Present as 'exe.' in 2022 official Tourist Visa Table (Tourists to Israel PDF) but was missing from the prior record.
ADDED Cook Islands (COK) to visa_free: Present as 'exe.' in 2022 official Tourist Visa Table but was missing from the prior record.
ADDED Central African Republic (CAF) to visa_free: Present as 'exe.' in 2022 official Tourist Visa Table but was missing from the prior record.

REMOVED Tuvalu (TUV) from visa_free: The 2022 official Tourist Visa Table (Tourists to Israel PDF) shows Tuvalu as 'req.' for both national and official passports. Incorrectly listed as visa_free in prior record.
REMOVED Marshall Islands (MHL) from visa_free: The 2022 official Tourist Visa Table shows Marshall Islands as 'req.' for national passport. Incorrectly listed as visa_free in prior record.
REMOVED Samoa (WSM) from visa_free: The 2022 official Tourist Visa Table shows Samoa as 'req.' for national passport. Incorrectly listed as visa_free in prior record.

UPDATED Kosovo (XKX) note: The 2022 official Tourist Visa Table shows Kosovo as 'req.' for national passport. Kosovo's exemption appears to have been granted approximately May 2025; entry retained in visa_free with updated note clarifying the timeline.
ADDED Bosnia Herzegovina (BIH) to conditional_access as a separate entry: 2022 Tourist Visa Table shows BIH national as 'req.' but official as 'exe.' — was missing from conditional_access.

CORRECTED Russia (RUS) notes: The 2022 official Tourist Visa Table shows Russian Federation national as 'exe.' with 'Requires MFA confirmation' note, and official as 'req.' — updated notes to reflect that official passports require a visa.
CORRECTED Belarus (BLR) notes: 2022 table shows national as 'exe.' but official as 'req.' — updated notes to reflect this asymmetry.
CORRECTED Dominica (DMA) notes: 2022 table shows national as 'exe.' but official as 'req.' — updated notes.
CORRECTED Saint Kitts and Nevis (KNA) notes: 2022 table shows national as 'exe.' but official as 'req.' — updated notes.
CORRECTED South Africa (ZAF) notes: 2022 table shows national as 'exe.' but official as 'req.' — updated notes.
CORRECTED Taiwan notes: 2022 table shows national as 'exe.' but official as 'req.' — updated notes.
CORRECTED United States notes: 2022 table shows national as 'exe.' but official as 'req.' — updated notes.
CORRECTED Egypt entry: removed classification as pure visa_free; clarified that the 14-day exemption requires MFA confirmation and has geographic/border restrictions.
UPDATED primary source_url references from gov.il/en/pages/tourist-visa-table (HTTP 403) to the directly scraped official PDFs.
UPDATED overall visa_policy notes to reflect official source dates and changes verified.
REMOVED first generic 'All visa-exempt country nationals' entry from visa_free array: not a specific nationality entry; ETA-IL details moved to eta sub-section only.
gaps: No credential-based conditional entry rules found for Israel: Israel does not grant visa-free or expedited entry to visa-required nationals solely because they hold a valid US, Schengen, UK, Canadian, Australian, or other third-country visa or residence permit. No such policy was found on any official Israeli government source.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or equivalent special-status cardholder entry exemption found for Israel on any official source.

The official Tourist Visa Table PDF for inbound tourists is dated 07.04.2022; an updated version was not found in PDF form for 2025/2026. Post-2022 changes (e.g., Seychelles, Colombia, Kosovo) are tracked from secondary cross-referenced sources.

The B/5 investor visa official embassy page (embassies.gov.il/san-francisco) was inaccessible during validation; details confirmed from search snippets referencing Government Resolutions 1528 and 2243.
Israel does not have a formal publicly accessible digital-nomad visa program as of research date.
The innovationisrael.org.il program page returned HTTP 403; program details confirmed from official government search snippets.
Israel generally requires dual citizenship renunciation upon naturalization; specific statutory details not confirmed from directly scraped official page.

Exact effective date for Kosovo visa exemption (for entry into Israel on ordinary passport) could not be confirmed from the official Israeli Tourist Visa Table, which still listed Kosovo as 'req.' in the 2022 PDF. Status noted as 'approximately May 2025' based on secondary sources.

Samoa (WSM), Tuvalu (TUV), and Marshall Islands (MHL) are corrected to visa-required per the 2022 official PDF; however a 2025/2026 updated inbound PDF could not be retrieved to confirm no changes since 2022.
